raw_text.question: In a rhombus, find the length of a side if the diagonals are 30 and 40.
raw_text.response: Okay, so we have in the rhombus with the length of a side, find the length of the side as the diagonals are 30 and 40. Mm-hmm. Okay, so I really like this task because I really like geometry. So basically we know that in the rhombus, all sides are equal. And we also know that in the rhombus, diagonals are perpendicular to each other. So I think we can use a Pythagoras theorem here. One of the, which one, of the diagonal, so 30 and other than 40, then they divide rhombus into four equal triangles with a side of 15 and 20. And then according to Pythagoras theorem, it's A square plus B square is equal to C square. And then we, this is how we understand it. Yeah, and then like 15 squared, 15 squared is, 20 squared is 400, 15 squared is 225, and we have 625, and this is square root of which gives us 25, so the length is 25.
